data.description:
## Why

Audit OSS finding #6 bucket (versioning honesty): Python 3.13 has been current-stable for well over a year, but the project tests and advertises only 3.11 and 3.12 — test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml matrices stop at 3.12, and pyproject classifiers list 3.11/3.12 only. For a published PyPI package this reads as either unsupported or untested on the interpreter most new environments default to.

## What / files

- .github/workflows/test.yml and .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml: add 3.13 to the Linux python-version matrix (keep the macOS single-version cost policy as documented)
- pyproject.toml: add the Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13 classifier once the matrix is green
- Fix any 3.13-specific failures the matrix surfaces (or, if a real blocker exists, document why 3.13 is excluded instead — the audit's alternative remedy)
- docs/api-stability.md / README supported-versions wording updated to match

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml matrices include 3.13 on Linux and both run green
- [ ] pyproject classifiers include Python :: 3.13; twine check passes
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green under a local 3.13 interpreter (or the blocker is documented and the classifier withheld)
- [ ] README/docs supported-version statements match the matrix
data.title: Add Python 3.13 to the CI matrix, classifiers, and supported-version docs
